# Café Vermut — E-commerce Database Schema

**Project:** Café Vermut online coffee shop
**Database:** Supabase / PostgreSQL
**Generated by:** database-developer skill

---

## Tables Overview

| Table | Purpose | RLS |
|---|---|---|
| `products` | Coffee products with weight variants | Public read, service role write |
| `orders` | Customer orders lifecycle | Owner only + service role |
| `order_items` | Line items within an order | Owner only (via order) + service role |

---

## Migration Files

### Migration 1 — Shared infrastructure (trigger function)

**File:** `supabase/migrations/20240101000001_create_update_updated_at_function.sql`

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Shared trigger function: update_updated_at
-- Must be created once before any table that uses it
-- ============================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
```

---

### Migration 2 — Products table

**File:** `supabase/migrations/20240101000002_create_products_table.sql`

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Create products table
-- Café Vermut: ground coffee products with weight variants
-- ============================================================

-- Enum for product weight variants
CREATE TYPE product_weight AS ENUM ('250g', '500g', '1kg');

CREATE TABLE products (
  id          uuid        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name        text        NOT NULL,
  description text,
  price_cents integer     NOT NULL CHECK (price_cents >= 0),
  weight_grams integer    NOT NULL CHECK (weight_grams > 0),
  variant     product_weight NOT NULL,
  is_active   boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at  timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at  timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- updated_at trigger
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON products
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();

-- ============================================================
-- Indexes
-- ============================================================

-- Partial index: filter active products (most common query pattern:
-- storefront only shows active products — avoids full table scan)
CREATE INDEX idx_products_active
  ON products(created_at DESC)
  WHERE is_active = true;

-- Index on variant: filter/group by weight variant in product listing
CREATE INDEX idx_products_variant ON products(variant);

-- Full-text search: name + description (search bar on storefront)
CREATE INDEX idx_products_search
  ON products
  USING gin(to_tsvector('spanish', name || ' ' || COALESCE(description, '')));

-- ============================================================
-- RLS — products are public read, service role write
-- ============================================================

ALTER TABLE products ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Anyone (anon, authenticated) can read active products
CREATE POLICY "Anyone can view products"
  ON products
  FOR SELECT
  USING (true);

-- Only service role (backend with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) can write
CREATE POLICY "Service role manages products"
  ON products
  FOR ALL
  USING (auth.role() = 'service_role');
```

---

### Migration 3 — Orders table

**File:** `supabase/migrations/20240101000003_create_orders_table.sql`

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Create orders table
-- Café Vermut: customer orders from checkout to delivery
-- ============================================================

CREATE TYPE order_status AS ENUM (
  'PENDING',
  'CONFIRMED',
  'SHIPPED',
  'DELIVERED',
  'CANCELLED'
);

CREATE TABLE orders (
  id                       uuid         PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id                  uuid         NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status                   order_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
  total_cents              integer      NOT NULL CHECK (total_cents >= 0),
  currency                 text         NOT NULL DEFAULT 'EUR',
  stripe_payment_intent_id text,
  created_at               timestamptz  NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at               timestamptz  NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- updated_at trigger
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON orders
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();

-- ============================================================
-- Indexes
-- ============================================================

-- FK index: user_id — PostgreSQL does not auto-index FK columns.
-- Used every time a customer fetches their order history.
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_id ON orders(user_id);

-- Status index: filter orders by status (admin dashboard, fulfillment queue)
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);

-- Time-series: newest orders first (default sort in customer history + admin list)
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_created_at ON orders(created_at DESC);

-- Composite: status + date — single index satisfies the most common admin query:
-- "show me all PENDING orders, newest first"
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status_created_at ON orders(status, created_at DESC);

-- Stripe lookup: confirm payment webhook needs to find order by payment intent id
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_stripe_payment_intent_id
  ON orders(stripe_payment_intent_id)
  WHERE stripe_payment_intent_id IS NOT NULL;

-- ============================================================
-- RLS — users see only their own orders
-- ============================================================

ALTER TABLE orders ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Owner: read own orders
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own orders"
  ON orders
  FOR SELECT
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Owner: create own orders (user_id must match authenticated user)
CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own orders"
  ON orders
  FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Owner: update own orders (e.g. cancellation from customer side)
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own orders"
  ON orders
  FOR UPDATE
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id)
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Owner: delete own orders (PENDING orders only — enforced at app layer)
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own orders"
  ON orders
  FOR DELETE
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Service role: full access for backend (webhooks, admin, fulfillment)
CREATE POLICY "Service role has full access to orders"
  ON orders
  FOR ALL
  USING (auth.role() = 'service_role');
```

---

### Migration 4 — Order items table

**File:** `supabase/migrations/20240101000004_create_order_items_table.sql`

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Create order_items table
-- Café Vermut: line items within an order (price snapshot at time of purchase)
-- ============================================================

CREATE TABLE order_items (
  id                  uuid    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id            uuid    NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  product_id          uuid    NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  quantity            integer NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
  price_cents_at_time integer NOT NULL CHECK (price_cents_at_time >= 0),
  created_at          timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at          timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- updated_at trigger
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON order_items
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();

-- ============================================================
-- Indexes
-- ============================================================

-- FK index: order_id — fetch all items for a given order (always queried together with order)
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_order_id ON order_items(order_id);

-- FK index: product_id — reverse lookup: which orders contain a given product?
-- Also needed for ON DELETE RESTRICT check performance.
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_product_id ON order_items(product_id);

-- Composite: order_id + product_id — prevents duplicate line items at DB level
-- and optimises "does this order already have this product?" check
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_order_items_order_product
  ON order_items(order_id, product_id);

-- ============================================================
-- RLS — items are readable only by the owner of the parent order
-- ============================================================

ALTER TABLE order_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Owner: read items that belong to their orders
-- Join through orders table to verify ownership
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own order items"
  ON order_items
  FOR SELECT
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

-- Owner: insert items into their own orders
CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own order items"
  ON order_items
  FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

-- Owner: update items in their own orders
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own order items"
  ON order_items
  FOR UPDATE
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  )
  WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

-- Owner: delete items from their own orders
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own order items"
  ON order_items
  FOR DELETE
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

-- Service role: full access for backend (checkout server actions, admin)
CREATE POLICY "Service role has full access to order items"
  ON order_items
  FOR ALL
  USING (auth.role() = 'service_role');
```

## Complete Migration SQL (single file, idempotent)

For environments where a single migration file is preferred, or for initial project setup:

**File:** `supabase/migrations/20240101000000_cafe_vermut_initial_schema.sql`

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Café Vermut — Initial Schema
-- E-commerce database: products, orders, order_items
-- Idempotent: safe to apply on a clean database
-- ============================================================

-- ============================================================
-- 0. Shared infrastructure
-- ============================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- ============================================================
-- 1. Enums
-- ============================================================

DO $$ BEGIN
  CREATE TYPE product_weight AS ENUM ('250g', '500g', '1kg');
EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object THEN NULL;
END $$;

DO $$ BEGIN
  CREATE TYPE order_status AS ENUM (
    'PENDING',
    'CONFIRMED',
    'SHIPPED',
    'DELIVERED',
    'CANCELLED'
  );
EXCEPTION WHEN duplicate_object THEN NULL;
END $$;

-- ============================================================
-- 2. products
-- ============================================================

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS products (
  id           uuid        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name         text        NOT NULL,
  description  text,
  price_cents  integer     NOT NULL CHECK (price_cents >= 0),
  weight_grams integer     NOT NULL CHECK (weight_grams > 0),
  variant      product_weight NOT NULL,
  is_active    boolean     NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at   timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at   timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_updated_at ON products;
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON products
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_products_active
  ON products(created_at DESC)
  WHERE is_active = true;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_products_variant
  ON products(variant);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_products_search
  ON products
  USING gin(to_tsvector('spanish', name || ' ' || COALESCE(description, '')));

ALTER TABLE products ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Anyone can view products" ON products;
CREATE POLICY "Anyone can view products"
  ON products FOR SELECT USING (true);

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Service role manages products" ON products;
CREATE POLICY "Service role manages products"
  ON products FOR ALL USING (auth.role() = 'service_role');

-- ============================================================
-- 3. orders
-- ============================================================

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
  id                       uuid         PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id                  uuid         NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status                   order_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',
  total_cents              integer      NOT NULL CHECK (total_cents >= 0),
  currency                 text         NOT NULL DEFAULT 'EUR',
  stripe_payment_intent_id text,
  created_at               timestamptz  NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at               timestamptz  NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_updated_at ON orders;
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON orders
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_orders_user_id
  ON orders(user_id);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_orders_status
  ON orders(status);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_orders_created_at
  ON orders(created_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_orders_status_created_at
  ON orders(status, created_at DESC);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_orders_stripe_payment_intent_id
  ON orders(stripe_payment_intent_id)
  WHERE stripe_payment_intent_id IS NOT NULL;

ALTER TABLE orders ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can view own orders" ON orders;
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own orders"
  ON orders FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can insert own orders" ON orders;
CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own orders"
  ON orders FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can update own orders" ON orders;
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own orders"
  ON orders FOR UPDATE
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id)
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can delete own orders" ON orders;
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own orders"
  ON orders FOR DELETE USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Service role has full access to orders" ON orders;
CREATE POLICY "Service role has full access to orders"
  ON orders FOR ALL USING (auth.role() = 'service_role');

-- ============================================================
-- 4. order_items
-- ============================================================

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS order_items (
  id                  uuid    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  order_id            uuid    NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  product_id          uuid    NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
  quantity            integer NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
  price_cents_at_time integer NOT NULL CHECK (price_cents_at_time >= 0),
  created_at          timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at          timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_updated_at ON order_items;
CREATE TRIGGER set_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON order_items
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at();

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_order_items_order_id
  ON order_items(order_id);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_order_items_product_id
  ON order_items(product_id);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_order_items_order_product
  ON order_items(order_id, product_id);

ALTER TABLE order_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can view own order items" ON order_items;
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own order items"
  ON order_items FOR SELECT
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can insert own order items" ON order_items;
CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own order items"
  ON order_items FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can update own order items" ON order_items;
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own order items"
  ON order_items FOR UPDATE
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  )
  WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Users can delete own order items" ON order_items;
CREATE POLICY "Users can delete own order items"
  ON order_items FOR DELETE
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM orders
      WHERE orders.id = order_items.order_id
        AND orders.user_id = auth.uid()
    )
  );

DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "Service role has full access to order items" ON order_items;
CREATE POLICY "Service role has full access to order items"
  ON order_items FOR ALL USING (auth.role() = 'service_role');
```

## Seed Data

**File:** `supabase/seed.sql`

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Café Vermut — Seed Data
-- Idempotent: ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on all inserts
-- Safe to run after every supabase db reset
-- NOTE: Does not include real user data, real orders, or payment records
-- ============================================================

-- ============================================================
-- Products — Ground coffee variants for Café Vermut
-- ============================================================

-- Ethiopia Yirgacheffe 250g
INSERT INTO products (id, name, description, price_cents, weight_grams, variant, is_active)
VALUES (
  '00000000-cafe-0001-0000-000000000001',
  'Ethiopia Yirgacheffe',
  'Café de origen único de las tierras altas de Etiopía. Notas de frutos del bosque, jazmín y cítricos con acidez brillante.',
  1250,
  250,
  '250g',
  true
)
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

  true
)
```

---

## Schema Documentation

### Table: `products`

**Purpose:** Coffee products catalogue. Each row represents a specific product + weight variant combination (e.g., "Ethiopia Yirgacheffe 500g" is a separate row from "Ethiopia Yirgacheffe 1kg").

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| id | uuid | NO | gen_random_uuid() | Primary key |
| name | text | NO | — | Product name (e.g., "Ethiopia Yirgacheffe") |
| description | text | YES | — | Long-form product description |
| price_cents | integer | NO | — | Price in euro cents (€12.50 = 1250) |
| weight_grams | integer | NO | — | Weight in grams (250, 500, 1000) |
| variant | product_weight | NO | — | Enum shorthand: '250g', '500g', '1kg' |
| is_active | boolean | NO | true | Whether product is visible in the storefront |
| created_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | Auto-set on insert |
| updated_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | Auto-updated on every change |

**Enum: `product_weight`**
- `250g` — 250 gram bag
- `500g` — 500 gram bag
- `1kg` — 1 kilogram bag

**Indexes:**
| Index | Type | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| `idx_products_active` | Partial B-tree on `created_at DESC WHERE is_active = true` | Storefront only shows active products; partial index avoids scanning inactive rows |
| `idx_products_variant` | B-tree on `variant` | Filter products by bag size |
| `idx_products_search` | GIN tsvector `(name, description)` | Full-text search bar on storefront (Spanish language) |

**RLS:** Enabled
- `Anyone can view products` — SELECT for all roles (anon, authenticated)
- `Service role manages products` — ALL for service_role (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE via backend only)

---

### Table: `orders`

**Purpose:** Records each customer order from checkout through delivery. Created when customer initiates payment; status progresses through the fulfilment lifecycle.

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| id | uuid | NO | gen_random_uuid() | Primary key |
| user_id | uuid | NO | — | FK → auth.users.id (CASCADE DELETE) |
| status | order_status | NO | 'PENDING' | Current order state |
| total_cents | integer | NO | — | Order total in euro cents (€22.00 = 2200) |
| currency | text | NO | 'EUR' | ISO 4217 currency code |
| stripe_payment_intent_id | text | YES | — | Stripe PaymentIntent ID for webhook matching |
| created_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | Auto-set on insert |
| updated_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | Auto-updated on every change |

**Enum: `order_status`**
- `PENDING` — Order created, awaiting payment confirmation
- `CONFIRMED` — Stripe webhook confirmed payment received
- `SHIPPED` — Shipping label generated, tracking active
- `DELIVERED` — Delivery confirmed
- `CANCELLED` — Order cancelled (before or after payment)

**Indexes:**
| Index | Type | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| `idx_orders_user_id` | B-tree on `user_id` | FK not auto-indexed; used on every customer order history fetch |
| `idx_orders_status` | B-tree on `status` | Admin dashboard filters by status |
| `idx_orders_created_at` | B-tree on `created_at DESC` | Default sort for order lists (newest first) |
| `idx_orders_status_created_at` | Composite B-tree on `(status, created_at DESC)` | Single index satisfies most common admin query: "PENDING orders, newest first" |
| `idx_orders_stripe_payment_intent_id` | Partial B-tree on `stripe_payment_intent_id WHERE NOT NULL` | Webhook handler looks up order by Stripe PI id; partial avoids indexing null rows |

**RLS:** Enabled
- `Users can view own orders` — SELECT WHERE user_id = auth.uid()
- `Users can insert own orders` — INSERT CHECK user_id = auth.uid()
- `Users can update own orders` — UPDATE WHERE/CHECK user_id = auth.uid()
- `Users can delete own orders` — DELETE WHERE user_id = auth.uid()
- `Service role has full access to orders` — ALL for service_role

**Relations:**
- `user_id` → `auth.users.id` (CASCADE DELETE — deleting user deletes their orders)
- Referenced by `order_items.order_id`

---

### Table: `order_items`

**Purpose:** Individual line items within an order. Stores `price_cents_at_time` as a snapshot — if the product price changes later, the historical order record remains correct.

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| id | uuid | NO | gen_random_uuid() | Primary key |
| order_id | uuid | NO | — | FK → orders.id (CASCADE DELETE) |
| product_id | uuid | NO | — | FK → products.id (RESTRICT DELETE) |
| quantity | integer | NO | — | Number of units ordered (> 0) |
| price_cents_at_time | integer | NO | — | Unit price at time of purchase in euro cents |
| created_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | Auto-set on insert |
| updated_at | timestamptz | NO | now() | Auto-updated on every change |

**Indexes:**
| Index | Type | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| `idx_order_items_order_id` | B-tree on `order_id` | FK not auto-indexed; fetching all items for an order is the primary read pattern |
| `idx_order_items_product_id` | B-tree on `product_id` | FK not auto-indexed; also used for ON DELETE RESTRICT check and product sales reports |
| `idx_order_items_order_product` | Unique B-tree on `(order_id, product_id)` | Prevents duplicate product rows in the same order; covers the exists-check query |

**RLS:** Enabled
- All policies use EXISTS subquery on `orders` table to verify ownership (items have no direct `user_id`)
- `Users can view own order items` — SELECT via EXISTS(orders WHERE user_id = auth.uid())
- `Users can insert own order items` — INSERT via EXISTS check
- `Users can update own order items` — UPDATE via EXISTS check
- `Users can delete own order items` — DELETE via EXISTS check
- `Service role has full access to order items` — ALL for service_role

**Relations:**
- `order_id` → `orders.id` (CASCADE DELETE — deleting an order deletes its items)
- `product_id` → `products.id` (RESTRICT DELETE — cannot delete a product that has been ordered)

---

## TypeScript Types (post-migration generation)

After applying migrations, run:

```bash
supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/database.types.ts
```

Expected generated types:

```ts
// src/types/database.types.ts — generated, never edit manually

export type Database = {
  public: {
    Tables: {
      products: {
        Row: {
          id: string
          name: string
          description: string | null
          price_cents: number
          weight_grams: number
          variant: "250g" | "500g" | "1kg"
          is_active: boolean
          created_at: string
          updated_at: string
        }
        Insert: {
          id?: string
          name: string
          description?: string | null
          price_cents: number
          weight_grams: number
          variant: "250g" | "500g" | "1kg"
          is_active?: boolean
          created_at?: string
          updated_at?: string
        }
        Update: {
          id?: string
          name?: string
          description?: string | null
          price_cents?: number
          weight_grams?: number
          variant?: "250g" | "500g" | "1kg"
          is_active?: boolean
          created_at?: string
          updated_at?: string
        }
      }
      orders: {
        Row: {
          id: string
          user_id: string
          status: "PENDING" | "CONFIRMED" | "SHIPPED" | "DELIVERED" | "CANCELLED"
          total_cents: number
          currency: string
          stripe_payment_intent_id: string | null
          created_at: string
          updated_at: string
        }
        Insert: {
          id?: string
          user_id: string
          status?: "PENDING" | "CONFIRMED" | "SHIPPED" | "DELIVERED" | "CANCELLED"
          total_cents: number
          currency?: string
          stripe_payment_intent_id?: string | null
          created_at?: string
          updated_at?: string
        }
        Update: {
          id?: string
          user_id?: string
          status?: "PENDING" | "CONFIRMED" | "SHIPPED" | "DELIVERED" | "CANCELLED"
          total_cents?: number
          currency?: string
          stripe_payment_intent_id?: string | null
          created_at?: string
          updated_at?: string
        }
      }
      order_items: {
        Row: {
          id: string
          order_id: string
          product_id: string
          quantity: number
          price_cents_at_time: number
          created_at: string
          updated_at: string
        }
        Insert: {
          id?: string
          order_id: string
          product_id: string
          quantity: number
          price_cents_at_time: number
          created_at?: string
          updated_at?: string
        }
        Update: {
          id?: string
          order_id?: string
          product_id?: string
          quantity?: number
          price_cents_at_time?: number
          created_at?: string
          updated_at?: string
        }
      }
    }
    Enums: {
      product_weight: "250g" | "500g" | "1kg"
      order_status: "PENDING" | "CONFIRMED" | "SHIPPED" | "DELIVERED" | "CANCELLED"
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Supabase CLI Commands

```bash
# Apply migrations to local Supabase instance
supabase db reset

# Push to remote
supabase db push

# Regenerate TypeScript types
supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/database.types.ts

# Verify RLS is enabled on all tables
psql -c "SELECT tablename, rowsecurity FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = 'public';"
```

---

## Design Decisions

### Why `product_weight` enum + `weight_grams` integer (both columns)?

The `variant` enum (`250g`, `500g`, `1kg`) is the business-facing label displayed to customers. The `weight_grams` integer is used for shipping calculations (carriers require exact weight). Having both avoids parsing strings and allows direct arithmetic in shipping queries.

### Why `price_cents_at_time` on `order_items`?

Product prices change over time (promotions, adjustments). Storing the price at purchase time in `order_items` means historical orders always display the correct amount paid. The `products.price_cents` reflects the current price; `order_items.price_cents_at_time` reflects what was actually charged.

### Why `ON DELETE RESTRICT` for `order_items.product_id`?

A product that appears in a past order cannot be deleted — it would corrupt the order record. Use `is_active = false` to hide products from the storefront. Only products with zero order history can be hard-deleted.

### Why `ON DELETE CASCADE` for `order_items.order_id`?

When an order is deleted (e.g., admin purge), its line items have no meaning in isolation. Cascade deletion maintains referential integrity without requiring manual cleanup.

### Why EXISTS subquery for `order_items` RLS instead of a direct `user_id`?

`order_items` has no `user_id` column — ownership is established through the `orders` table. The EXISTS subquery is the correct pattern. The `idx_order_items_order_id` + `idx_orders_user_id` indexes ensure this subquery is efficient.

### Why partial index on `stripe_payment_intent_id`?

Most orders in state PENDING may not yet have a Stripe PI assigned (or it may be null during checkout initiation). Indexing only non-null values keeps the index smaller and faster for the webhook handler lookup.
